mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of RSAFS versus 20-day SPX returns is consistently below zero or not significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, it indicates monetary tightening that reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and dampened economic activity, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS>5.0%, the median 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05) or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based demand strength that robustly supports equity markets, leading to positive returns over a quarterly horizon as economic momentum builds.
failure_condition: When both series rise month-over-month, the forward 60-day SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or the average return is less than 1%.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market with unemployment below 5%, further declines in UNRATE signal increasing labor cost pressures that compress profit margins and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE<5%, the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative, or the Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding output and capacity utilization, which is associated with higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The rolling 60-month correlation of INDPRO percentage changes with forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r<0.1) or the Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds the threshold level of 210, it signals elevated inflation that prompts monetary tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In months when CPIAUCSL>210, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10) or more than 50% of returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in GDP (GDPC1) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate that growth is occurring under tightening monetary conditions, which typically depresses equity valuations as rising rates outweigh fundamental strength, leading to negative returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS rise quarter-over-quarter, the forward 5-day SPX return is not negative on average (t-test p>0.10) or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 2%), increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal robust residential investment that stimulates the economy and supports positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS<2%, the correlation between HOUST changes and forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive, or the Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window is below 0.03.
